observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the:
a. concerned supplier to deliver the remaining undelivered office
supplies totaling ₱523,810.00, otherwise, pursue to terminate the
contract, notwithstanding the consideration of blacklisting of the
latter due to unsatisfactory progress in the delivery of the goods
arising from his fault or negligence in accordance with Appendix 17
of the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
b. Municipal Accountant to (a) impose the appropriate amount of
liquidated damages to the supplier based on the date of the
termination of the contract and/or complete delivery of the
supplier; and (b) provide the necessary journal entry to record the
running liquidated damages amounting to ₱237,285.93 as of year-
end.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management require the
Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation and make the necessary
adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct
balances of the Due from LGUs account and the related payable accounts in the
books of the concerned barangays as at December 31, 2024.

We recommended that the Management to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. coordinate with the offices of the MSWDO and MDRRMO and compel
them to submit the necessary documents relative to the issuance of the
welfare goods totaling ₱11,303,851.90 and require the submission of the
list of recipients duly acknowledged by them;
b. prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the issuances of
welfare goods totaling ₱11,303,851.90; and
c. ensure expenses for inventories are recognized when the goods are
distributed or the related service is rendered.

We recommended that the Management instruct:
a. the end-users to (i) consider several factors such as the market
prices of goods procured from various known suppliers in
consonance with Sections 7.3.2 and 36 of RIRR of RA No. 9184 to
avoid bloated ABC which are being used by the bidders as guide in
quoting; and (ii) provide justification for the procurement of an
overpriced and outdated laptop computers and overpriced heavy
equipment instead of that with higher specifications that could be
purchased within the ABC;
b. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to impose
liquidated damages in the amount of ₱196,000.00 against Felta
Multi-Media, Inc. for delay in the delivery of procured laptop
computers; and
c. Municipal Accountant to derecognize the book value of the 61 units
laptop computers upon transfer of its absolute ownership through
the issuance of Deed of Donation and property transfer report.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management require the
Inventory Committee to:
a) conduct physical count of inventory items by type in accordance with
Section 124 Volume 1 of the MNGAS and Section 156 of COA Circular
No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992;
b) prepare RPCI and submit the duly signed copy of RPCI to COA not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semester, respectively, pursuant to Annex 48, Volume II of the NGAS
Manual; and
c) exert effort in determining the status of the items which remained
recorded as inventories despite being unaccounted for as long as 5
years.

incorporating excessive quantity of materials in the Program of

entry to record the excess materials amounting to ₱496,090.00 as
Construction Materials Inventory; and
c. Municipal Inspectorate Committee to prove that the construction
materials received per Purchase Order No. 101-2408-720 dated
August 28, 2024 were complete.

We recommended that the Management instruct the:
a. OIC-MPDO and Supply Officer to notify the Supplier in writing
of their claims arising under the warranty for the subject
procurement and require them to repair or replace the defective
parts thereof without cost to the Procuring Entity in accordance
with Section IV(5) of the GCC, otherwise, proceed to call upon the
warranty security, without prejudice to any other rights which the
municipality may have against the supplier under the contract and
under the applicable law.
b. Municipal Inspectorate Committee to (i) inspect all the distributed
haval motor vehicles and report any discovered defects and
deficiencies to the Supply Officer and OIC-MPDO for the proper
claiming of warranty and (ii) provide the Audit Team of their
appropriate inspection report thereof;
c. OIC-MPDO to (i) facilitate the issuance of the Deed of Donation,
with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan, for the haval motor
vehicles distributed to the barangays of the municipality; and (ii)
remind the recipient barangays to use the subject haval motor
vehicles only for solid waste management;
d. Supply Officer revoke the Invoice-Receipt for Property issued to
the concerned barangays upon execution/issuance of Deed of
Donation and MOA; and
e. Municipal Accountant to derecognize the book value of the
procured motor vehicles upon transfer of its absolute ownership
through the issuance of Deed of Donation.

We recommended the Management to direct the:
a) Municipal Inventory Committee to (i) prepare and submit the
RPCPPE for the year; and (ii) consider to conduct the one-time
cleansing of the PPE in order to establish the PPE balances that are
verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and

entries for (i) the recognition of depreciation for their Road

consideration, IPSAS 3 - Accounting Policies, Changes in
Accounting Estimates and Errors; and (ii) reclassification of Road
Networks totaling ₱366,800.00 to its proper account.

We recommended the Management to instruct the:

entry to recognize the unremitted GSIS premium contributions
totaling ₱161,028.19; and

RIRR of RA No. 8291; and (ii) strictly monitor the remittance of
withheld amounts to prevent accumulation and incurrence of
unnecessary penalties.

Municipal Vice Mayor and Office of the Sangguniang Bayan to immediately
stop the hiring of individuals under Job Order charged out of the
appropriations of the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan, that would be assigned
to other government agencies.

We recommended that the Management to stop the procurement,
obligation and payment of expenses that are in violation of the Prohibition
Against Expenses for Reception and Entertainment under Section 343 of RA
No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management to instruct the:
a. OIC-Municipal Engineer and OIC-MPDC to demand the
concerned contractor to extend the validity of the performance
bond; and

the subject infrastructure project and by monitoring the

revised catch-up plan and contract expiry date; (ii) submit the
supporting documents for the Project Extension Agreement dated
April 1, 2024.

We recommended that the Local School Board:
a. ensure that the copy of the SEF Budget is furnished the Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer
within seven (7) days from its approval; and
b. provide a copy of the quarterly and annual SEF report (SEF
Budget Accountability Form No. 1) to the Regional Offices of the
DBM and DILG for the current and ensuing years.

We recommended that the Management:
a. re-evaluate the composition of the amount invested in time deposit
accounts and revert to the respective current accounts all amounts
earmarked for specific PPAs to ensure funds appropriated for
these projects are readily available for utilization;
b. compel the Municipal Treasurer to revert the portion of fund
invested for implementation of projects to current account;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit their cash
analysis to the LFC, LCE and SB, in order to update the actual
available and idle funds of the municipality that may be invested
in HYSA/Time Deposits; and
d. coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to update the existing
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing the Municipal
Treasurer to invest municipal funds in High-Yield Savings
Accounts (HYSA) and/or Time Deposits and explicitly specify the
maximum allowable amount for investment, the duration or term
of the investment, and the target interest rate which should be duly
supported by the cash analysis of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Management:
a. require the concerned SB Members to (i) return the excess training and
travelling expenses claimed amounting to ₱42,250.00; and (ii) submit the
certification by the agency head or authorized representative relative to
the absolute necessity of their actual accommodation expenses; and
b. ensure that subsequent claims for travelling and training expenses are
within the purview set under NBC No. 563 and EO No. 77.

We recommended that the Management:
a) maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and
implement the development projects within the targeted
timeframe to achieve the desirable socio-economic and
environmental outcomes of the Municipality;
b) review unimplemented PPAs to ascertain viability of their
implementation otherwise reprogram including those balances
from completed projects that need to re-appropriate for a more
viable and significant PPAs in accordance to the socio-economic
development and environmental management requirements
provided under Sections 287 and 322 of RA No. 7160; and
c) refrain from charging ineligible expenses out the 20%
Development Fund.

We recommended the Management to require the OIC-MDRRMO to:
a. increase the efforts in maximizing the utilization of its DRRM
Fund for the implementation of the PPAs through proper
planning, strict monitoring, and fast track the implementation of
the DRRM PPAs in order to strengthen and enhance disaster
preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality to
respond to emergencies or disasters; and
b. consider the full utilization of the cash donations received for
DRRM in accordance with the purpose of the grant.

payable and income accounts in the books at year-end; and

We recommended the Management to require:
the Municipal Accounting Office and the Local Finance Committee to provide the Audit Team of the actions undertaken from the time the operation of the waterworks ceased up to this date particularly on the funds transferred from the GF-Proper to the waterworks which remained unliquidated to this date; and
the Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjustments on the i) Due from LEE account to reflect only amounts collectible to existing and operational local economic enterprise of the Municipality; and ii) Other Payable account to reflect only amounts of valid obligations of the Municipality.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendations that the:
Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs owned by the Municipality and render a complete RPCPPE not later than the prescribed date of submission;
Municipal Accounting Office and the Inventory Committee to conduct reconciliation of their records to correct the reported balances of PPEs at year-end, any discrepancies between the RPCPPE and the Accounting Records should be investigated and adjusted accordingly; and
General Services Officer (GSO)/MTO in the absence of GSO, account all identified unserviceable properties to facilitate the preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and other supporting documents for its disposal; and consider the immediate disposal of its unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration of the property, generate additional funds out of it and to relieve the responsible official of accountability over said properties.

For the above observations raised, we recommended the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to (i) settle all valid claims against the Municipal Government for terminal leave benefits due to officers and employees who had already retired, voluntarily resigned or separated from service which should be timely and expeditious release or paid  as mandated in RA No. 10154; and b) If no actual claim has been filed to said payables, facilitate reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

For the above observation raised, we recommended the Management to:
require the Municipal Accounting Office to effect necessary adjustments, both in the books of the General Fund Proper and the MHDC to reflect the correct balances of Hospital Fees of MHDC and Donation account in the General Fund Proper; and refrain from recognizing said costs as income in the books of the Municipality for lack of legal basis;
adhere with proper classification and description of accounts as presented in the Revised Chart of Accounts of LGUs to reflect correct balances of the corresponding accounts in the financial statements, and on the appropriate treatment of funds transferred from the General Fund Proper to its special accounts maintained; and
exclude the amount under hospital fees (non-cash) in the computation of total receipts/resources available for appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures:

Cause the nullification of the Purchase Order/Contract entered into between the Municipal Government of Boac and Inland Corporation due to the failure of the latter to fulfill what it provides, the foremost of which is the delivery of a brand new garbage compactor that has to be free of any defect and be reliably utilized in the garbage collection activities of the local government unit; and

Adhere strictly to the provision of law, specifically Section 23 of Republic Act 9003 relative to the collection of solid wastes that are being undertaken by the cities/municipalities.
observations.managementAction: The management stressed out that the reliability of the service in solid waste management is more important than the type of collection vehicles used. The Municipal Accountant stated that the Municipal Mayor signed/issued a Final Nullification Notice on May 13, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer or the Supply/Administrative Officer to see to it that in the procurement of goods, the corresponding Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) conforms with the PBDs; and

Direct the above Officer, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the preparation of the ABC for every procurement of infrastructure activity in accordance with the provisions of the foregoing department order and its attachment; and

Ensure that the subject document embodies the realistic quantities and costs based on the existing rates and estimates in order for the local government unit to conform with the guidelines set forth on this matter.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC stated that upon his assumption as BAC Chairman last January 2016, the Supply Officer had ensured that the corresponding ABC in the procurement of goods conforms with the Philippine Bidding Documents. He also stated that the Municipal Engineer had ensured the preparation of the ABC for the Contract in all procurement of infrastructure activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management the following:

Hold the contractor liable for any structural defects found in its technical inspection due to the lack of experience of the contractor to undertake the vertical construction of a two-storey commercial building. Otherwise, hold the BAC liable for failure to validate the contractor’s competence and experience as provided in Section 23.5.2.5 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and

After proper correction of structural defects noted, devise measures and appropriate courses of action to find ways and means to facilitate the opening for business of the new public market/commercial building inasmuch as it was constructed to strengthen and invigorate the commercial activities in the municipality; and

Make representations with the Vice-Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan for their appropriate action relative to the enactment of an ordinance for its operation; and

Ensure that the purpose for the construction of the subject structure will be served to the public for them to have business opportunities and the municipal government to generate income out of its operation that is very vital for the delivery of services and implementation of worthwhile projects.
observations.managementAction: The New Market - already operational and providing appropriate income.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures:

Disqualify the bidder/s who failed to comply/deficient with the eligibility and documentary requirements and has no legal or technical capability to undergo or prosecute a project to the detriment of the procuring agency hence, the transaction may be disallowed in audit;

Cause the institution of appropriate administrative action against the BAC members/officials for being negligent of the requirements of the law  and for being responsible in the mis-recommendation for the awarding of the contract; and

Require the BAC to comply strictly with the requirements of RA 9184 in its procurement activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:

Make concrete efforts by sending demand letters to the concerned barangays for them to be reminded of their obligation for the immediate settlement of their outstanding balances and improve the present monitoring and control measures on the liquidation thereof;

Remind or require them to immediately prepare fund utilization report and have the unused balance of fund transfer acknowledged by the municipality through the issuance of official receipt by the Municipal Treasurer, after which the report should be submitted/forwarded to the Office of the Municipal Accountant so that the settlement/liquidation could be recorded in the books of accounts; and

For future grants of fund transfers to barangays, strict adherence to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 3, 1994 is enjoined to ensure prompt settlement/liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management stressed out that the reliability of the service in solid waste management is more important than the type of collection vehicles used. The Municipal Accountant reported that as of April 30, 2016, the respective accounts of Brgy. Tanza and Bunganay were already liquidated reducing the balance by P50,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measures:

Compel the accountable officers concerned to immediately liquidate their cash advances; otherwise, demand full payment of the amount advanced to them;

Issue demand letters to the remaining accountable officers that were not yet issued with the same relative to their outstanding cash advances to comply with the provisions of the circular; and

Inform them that failure on their part to comply herewith shall constitute a ground for the withholding of the payment of the money due them as provided for by COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and a “prima facie” presumption under Article 217 of the Revised Penal Code that they have malversed the funds to their personal interest and benefit (Martinez vs. Sandiganbayan, GR No. 126413 dated August 10, 1999). Their failure shall otherwise constitute violation of Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code for failure to render accounts and of Section 89 of PD 1445 which are punishable under Section 128 of the same code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office noted that a demand letter was sent to one of the officials and the withholding of salary was effected on December, 2015. In another development, they cited that as of March 22, 2016 the various cash advances referred to above were already liquidated by the respective accountable officers which was validated by the Audit Team to be true.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to:

Look into the subject matter in a positive perspective in the end that this audit observation will serve as a valuable guide and reference in LGU’s procurement undertakings particularly in the conduct of public bidding; and

Ensure that the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 relative to the composition of the local Bids and Awards Committee are strictly adhered to by the concerned departments, bodies and officials of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor commented that they have already reorganized their BAC based on Exec. Order No. 2016-01 dated Jan. 5, 2016, wherein the SB Secretary is no longer a member of the BAC.

For issuance on Notice of Disallowance

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the following recommendations:

Adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to ensure efficient output, come up with projects with developmental impact that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the greater number of its constituents; and

Identify, first and foremost, the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan.  For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment of the latter to those that address the needs of the constituents as far as tangible projects and programs are concerned.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC gave more attention on the expenditures not covered by the 20% CDF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 7 in Part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: One of the reasons for the losses being incurred by the LEE is the Revenue Code of 1996 which has a lower fees and charges as compared to other municipalities. SB is currently conducting public hearings for the revision/ updating of revenue code; Slaughterhouse code was duly approved; Market code for new building was duly approved; MHO had implemented costing of services rendered by MHO officials and employees.
